message:
fix(abi): correct ctype bit layout to match glibc, add mmap fallback, and fix env reentry

ctype_abi: Fix character classification bitmask constants to match
glibc's actual x86_64 layout where property groups span the low and
high bytes non-sequentially (e.g., _ISUPPER=0x0100, _ISLOWER=0x0200,
_ISBLANK=0x0001). The previous sequential layout (1<<0 through 1<<11)
produced incorrect classification results for the __ctype_b_loc table.

malloc_abi: When the static bump heap is exhausted, fall back to
mmap(MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS) via raw syscall instead of returning
NULL. This prevents early startup OOM when the bump arena is consumed
before the membrane pipeline is initialized.

stdlib_abi: Add reentry guards to native_setenv and native_unsetenv
using AtomicBool CAS. Our setenv/unsetenv exports shadow the host's
versioned glibc symbols, so native_*_sym can resolve back to our
own functions causing infinite recursion. On reentry, setenv silently
succeeds and unsetenv falls back to remove_from_environ which directly
manipulates the environ array.

wchar_abi: Remove unused OsStrExt import, fix SYS_openat/SYS_readlinkat
casts to use implicit type instead of explicit `as i64`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
